title: Campus Improvement Plans
description: Campus Improvement Plans policy governs the development, content, and implementation of plans designed to improve student performance and school operations at the campus level. It outlines the processes and requirements for creating these plans, including the involvement of campus-level committees in the planning process.
description_local: Campus Improvement Plans (CIPs) are annual planning documents that outline each school's goals, strategies, and programs for improving student performance and overall school quality. These plans apply to every campus in the district and are shaped by input from teachers, parents, community members, and school staff. For parents and community members, this policy matters because it ensures there is a structured, transparent process for setting school priorities — and that the school board formally reviews and approves these plans each year.
